event: Middle Paleolithic hominins inhabit caves at Gajtan and other sites in present-day Albania, marking the earliest human presence.
Neolithic farming communities establish settlements such as Maliq and Vashtëmi in the lake basins of southeastern Albania.
Bronze Age tumulus-building cultures emerge, laying the foundations of the Illyrian peoples in the western Balkans.
Greek colonists from Corinth and Corcyra found Epidamnos (later Dyrrachium, modern Durrës) on the Adriatic coast.
Greek settlers found the colony of Apollonia near the Vjosa River, which becomes a major center of Hellenic culture.
Illyrian tribal kingdoms consolidate, with rulers such as Bardylis uniting tribes into a powerful state.
Queen Teuta of the Ardiaei rules the Illyrian kingdom, whose maritime raids draw Rome into conflict.
Rome launches the First Illyrian War against Queen Teuta, establishing its first foothold in the region.
Rome defeats King Gentius and dissolves the Illyrian kingdom, bringing the territory under Roman control.
The region is fully incorporated into the Roman Empire as part of the province of Illyricum.
The Via Egnatia, the great Roman road linking Dyrrachium to Byzantium, flourishes as a vital trade and military route.
Upon the division of the Roman Empire, the territory falls under the Eastern Roman (Byzantine) Empire.
Slavic and other migrating peoples raid Illyrian and Byzantine lands during the upheavals of late antiquity.
Progon establishes the Principality of Arbanon, the first known Albanian autonomous state under Byzantine suzerainty.
Charles of Anjou proclaims the Kingdom of Albania (Regnum Albaniae) centered on Durrës.
Albanian and Balkan lords appeal to the Ottomans, who begin extending their influence after the Battle of Savra.
Gjergj Kastrioti Skanderbeg raises the Albanian banner at Krujë and launches a prolonged resistance against the Ottomans.
Skanderbeg forms the League of Lezhë, uniting Albanian princes against Ottoman expansion.
Skanderbeg dies, and Albanian resistance gradually collapses as the Ottomans tighten their grip.
The Ottomans capture Krujë and Shkodër, completing their conquest and beginning over four centuries of rule.
Ali Pasha of Tepelena, the semi-independent Ottoman governor of Ioannina, is killed after defying the Sultan.
The League of Prizren is founded to defend Albanian-inhabited lands and promote national autonomy.
The Congress of Manastir standardizes the Albanian alphabet using the Latin script, advancing national identity.
Ismail Qemali proclaims Albania's independence in Vlorë during the First Balkan War.
The Treaty of London and the Conference of Ambassadors recognize an independent Albania while assigning Kosovo to Serbia.
Prince Wilhelm of Wied briefly rules as monarch before fleeing amid revolts and the outbreak of World War I.
The Congress of Lushnjë reasserts independence, makes Tirana the capital, and Albania joins the League of Nations.
Ahmet Zogu becomes president, consolidating power after suppressing his rivals.
Ahmet Zogu proclaims himself King Zog I, transforming Albania into a monarchy.
Fascist Italy invades and annexes Albania, forcing King Zog into exile.
Nazi Germany occupies Albania after Italy's capitulation, intensifying the communist-led partisan resistance.
Communist-led partisans under Enver Hoxha liberate Albania and seize power.
The People's Republic of Albania is proclaimed, with Enver Hoxha as the dominant communist leader.
Albania breaks with Yugoslavia and aligns closely with the Soviet Union.
Albania severs ties with the Soviet Union over de-Stalinization and forges a close alliance with Maoist China.
Hoxha declares Albania the world's first officially atheist state, banning all religious practice.
Albania breaks with China, plunging into extreme isolation as a self-reliant Stalinist state.
Enver Hoxha dies after four decades in power, and Ramiz Alia succeeds him as leader.
Mass protests and an exodus of refugees force the regime to legalize opposition parties and ease restrictions.
Albania holds its first multiparty elections, ending one-party communist rule and renaming itself the Republic of Albania.
The Democratic Party wins elections and Sali Berisha becomes president, accelerating the transition to a market economy.
The collapse of fraudulent pyramid investment schemes triggers nationwide anarchy and armed unrest.
Albania shelters hundreds of thousands of Kosovar Albanian refugees during the Kosovo War.
Albania joins NATO and formally applies for European Union membership.
Albania is granted official EU candidate country status.
Edi Rama's Socialist Party wins a decisive majority, continuing EU-oriented reforms and a justice system overhaul.
A powerful earthquake near Durrës kills dozens and causes widespread destruction across central Albania.
Albania formally opens European Union accession negotiations.
